title: Add finalizer for OpenBao PushSecret cleanup
summary: The OpenBao finalizer (keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer) is installed on first reconcile alongside the existing CC-0078 MariaDB finalizer, with both AddFinalizer paths returning Requeue=true so the next pass observes the persisted finalizer. Reconcile branches on DeletionTimestamp before any sub-reconciler runs and dispatches to both reconcileDelete and reconcileDeleteOpenBao in a single pass. finalizeOpenBaoSecrets issues Delete then a follow-up Get on each backup PushSecret (fernet-keys-backup, credential-keys-backup) and only reports done=true when both are confirmed NotFound, with apierrors.IsNotFound tolerated on both calls to make the handler idempotent. A PushSecret held Terminating by ESO's cleanup finalizer keeps the Keystone CR alive, requeues at RequeueSecretPolling, and surfaces SecretsReady=False / OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked naming the stuck PushSecret. Two Normal events are emitted once per termination: FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets (gated by hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets so requeues against a Terminating PushSecret do not re-emit) and OpenBaoSecretsFinalized. Both PushSecret builders set Spec.DeletionPolicy=PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete so ESO purges the kv-v2 paths on Delete. Coverage is thorough: 7 unit tests for finalizeOpenBaoSecrets and the DeletionPolicy builder, 9 Reconcile-level tests including SkipsSubReconcilers (CC-0078) and a 2-pass NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue, 2 envtest integration tests with real ESO finalizer simulation, and the deletion-cleanup chainsaw test extended to assert both finalizers on the live CR, both backup PushSecrets absent post-delete, and `bao kv get` against both kv-v2 paths returning non-zero. Docs: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md gains a 305-line OpenBao Finalizer subsection naming both KV-v2 paths, the wiring through ESO's DeletionPolicy=Delete, the asymmetry vs. the MariaDB finalizer, and the upgrade path. go vet clean, golangci-lint clean (0 issues), targeted finalizer test selection (TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets|TestReconcile_*OpenBaoFinalizer*|TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_*) passes. One WARNING: TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue is a tautological constant test that the external review pattern reject-tautological-constant-tests has flagged twice (CC-0067, CC-0078) and which has been deleted from sibling tests (TestKeystoneFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue from CC-0078 was removed for the same reason).

tests_checklist.item: Targeted finalizer/event/DeletionPolicy unit tests pass (go test -run 'TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets|TestReconcile_TerminatingCR|TestReconcile_*OpenBaoFinalizer*|Test*PushSecret_HasDeletionPolicyDelete|TestReconcileFernetKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete|TestReconcileCredentialKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete')
go vet clean on operators/keystone/...
golangci-lint clean on operators/keystone/internal/controller (0 issues)
Finalizer constant defined and used by all sites (REQ-005)
AddFinalizer on first reconcile with Requeue=true (TestReconcile_AddsOpenBaoFinalizerOnFirstReconcile)
Idempotent AddFinalizer with no extra Update (TestReconcile_OpenBaoFinalizerAlreadyPresent_NoExtraUpdate)
Update-conflict propagation covered (TestReconcile_OpenBaoFinalizerUpdateConflict_ReturnsError)

Terminating CR skips sub-reconcilers (TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_SkipsSubReconcilers — pre-existing CC-0078, asserts no SecretsReady/FernetKeysReady/CredentialKeysReady/DatabaseReady/NetworkPolicyReady set on terminating CR)
RequeueAfter while a PushSecret is Terminating (TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_RequeuesWhilePushSecretsExist + TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_RequeuesWhilePushSecretTerminating)
No-finalizer terminating CR is no-op for openbao path (TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_WithoutOpenBaoFinalizer_NoOp)
FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets and OpenBaoSecretsFinalized events asserted (TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingOpenBaoSecretsEvent, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsOpenBaoSecretsFinalizedEvent)
Exactly-once start-event semantics across requeues (TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue runs Reconcile twice and asserts FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets count == 1)
finalizeOpenBaoSecrets: delete-both / NotFound (3-case table) / idempotent / requeue-while-Terminating / blocked-condition all covered
OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked condition asserts Status/Reason/Message-contains-PushSecret-name/ObservedGeneration (TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_SetsBlockedConditionOnStall)
DeletionPolicy=Delete builder-level asserts on both PushSecret builders (TestFernetKeysPushSecret_HasDeletionPolicyDelete, TestCredentialKeysPushSecret_HasDeletionPolicyDelete) (REQ-008)

DeletionPolicy=Delete sub-reconciler-level asserts (TestReconcileFernetKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete, TestReconcileCredentialKeys_PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete) catch a future rewrite that bypasses the builder
envtest integration covers full lifecycle add/remove with simulated ESO cleanup finalizer (TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove)
envtest integration covers blocked-condition path with stuck PushSecret (TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizer_BlockedWhenPushSecretStuck)
Chainsaw E2E asserts both finalizers present on live CR via JMESPath contains() (REQ-010)
Chainsaw E2E asserts both backup PushSecrets absent (NotFound) after CR delete
Chainsaw E2E execs into openbao-0 and asserts `bao kv get` returns non-zero for both kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet-keys,credential-keys} paths (REQ-010)
Tests use NewGomegaWithT and FakeRecorder per established patterns
envtest tests gated by testutil.SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable per pattern

code_quality_checklist.item: keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer defined as a single package-level constant (single source of truth for Reconcile, handler, tests, docs) (REQ-005)
openBaoBackupPushSecretNames helper centralizes the two backup names (single source of truth — adding a third backup is a one-line change, mirrors mariaDBResourceCtors)
Reconcile dispatches both finalizer handlers when DeletionTimestamp is set, before any sub-reconciler runs (REQ-006)
AddFinalizer paths return Requeue=true so the next pass observes the persisted finalizer
reconcileDeleteOpenBao is a no-op if openbao finalizer is absent (REQ-001 brownfield path)
RemoveFinalizer only called after finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns done=true (REQ-002, REQ-004)
CC-0079 referenced in code comments, log lines, docs, chainsaw test, and integration tests

Godoc on reconcileDeleteOpenBao, finalizeOpenBaoSecrets, hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets, and setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition explains the WHY (event-dedupe sentinel, idempotency, blocked-condition rationale)
No unrelated refactoring in diff (only new finalizer code, two PushSecret builder additions, two test files extended, docs section appended, chainsaw test extended)
Constant naming -finalizer suffix differentiates from the MariaDB keystoneFinalizer when both appear under metadata.finalizers
log.FromContext(ctx).WithValues includes keystone name+namespace per include-correlation-fields-in-operator-log-entries pattern

security_checklist.item: Operator does not need or use OpenBao credentials — remote purge delegated to ESO via PushSecret.Spec.DeletionPolicy
Both PushSecret builders set DeletionPolicy=PushSecretDeletionPolicyDelete so ESO purges kv-v2/openstack/keystone/{fernet-keys,credential-keys} on Delete (REQ-008)
Finalizer guarantees ordering: Keystone CR removed only after both PushSecrets confirmed NotFound, preventing orphaned Fernet/credential key material in OpenBao
PushSecret names are derived from keystone.Name (not hardcoded), so multi-instance-per-namespace deployments cannot cross-delete each other's backups
kv-v2 path naming limitation (cluster-global rather than {name}-scoped) is documented in the Known Limitation note in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md
RBAC for PushSecret get/list/delete is already granted by the existing pre-CC-0079 ensure-PushSecret path (no new privileges needed for the finalizer)
NotFound tolerance prevents deletion deadlocks (idempotent on operator restart or manual SRE intervention)

architecture_checklist.item: Two parallel finalizers (MariaDB CC-0078, OpenBao CC-0079) are independent: each installed/tracked/released by its own handler, can complete in either order, neither blocks the other

Asymmetry with MariaDB finalizer is justified: OpenBao must wait for ESO to fully purge the kv-v2 path because the remote resource has no owner reference, while MariaDB resources have their own finalizers and owner references

Solution is as simple as possible — single new handler (reconcileDeleteOpenBao), single new cleanup function (finalizeOpenBaoSecrets), one helper for live-cleanup probe, one helper for blocked-condition
done=true requires confirmed NotFound on BOTH PushSecrets, not just successful Delete (REQ-003, REQ-004) — the re-Get is the load-bearing check

FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets sentinel driven by live PushSecret state via hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets (excludes Terminating PushSecrets so requeues do not re-emit), giving exactly-once event semantics across requeue loops
Brownfield path is implicit: NotFound tolerance + finalizer-absent fast-path together yield no events and no work for CRs created before CC-0079 with no PushSecrets ever provisioned
RequeueAfter uses the existing RequeueSecretPolling constant — no magic number, consistent with reconcileSecrets (CC-0044, CC-0047)

SecretsReady is reused (rather than introducing a new condition type) so any secret-store lifecycle concern surfaces under the same condition — coherent status surface across CC-0013, CC-0047, CC-0079

Documentation includes a Reconcile branching diagram, the DeletionPolicy=Delete wiring example, and explicit Owner-References-vs-Finalizer comparison table — supports validate-stated-acceptance-criteria-are-met and check-documentation-for-internal-contradictions patterns

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated PushSecret-name lists — openBaoBackupPushSecretNames is the single source of truth, used by both finalizeOpenBaoSecrets and hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets
No speculative abstractions — handler is purpose-built for the two backup PushSecrets; future third backup is a one-line change to openBaoBackupPushSecretNames
setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition extracted to keep finalizeOpenBaoSecrets narrow without over-engineering — one caller, one responsibility, mirrors helpers in sibling reconcilers

Two parallel paths (MariaDB and OpenBao finalizers) intentionally NOT extracted into a generic finalizer abstraction — the cleanup semantics differ (MariaDB does not wait, OpenBao does), abstraction would obscure the asymmetry

DeletionPolicy=Delete added to both fernet/credential PushSecret builders rather than introducing a shared 'backupPushSecret' helper — sibling builders already exist and the change is two lines each (deduplicate-logic-when-adding-parallel-templates was considered; existing parallel structure is acceptable for two callers)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Non-NotFound errors from Delete and Get propagate from finalizeOpenBaoSecrets so controller-runtime retries with backoff
AddFinalizer Update errors propagate (TestReconcile_OpenBaoFinalizerUpdateConflict_ReturnsError covers Conflict path)
RemoveFinalizer Update errors are wrapped with 'removing openbao finalizer' context for diagnosability
hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets propagates non-NotFound Get errors with PushSecret key context
No silent fallbacks — every error path either returns the error or matches the explicit NotFound tolerance pattern documented in finalizeOpenBaoSecrets godoc

defensive_checklist.item: No defensive checks beyond what production behavior requires — finalizer trusts apierrors.IsNotFound semantics rather than re-Get-confirming after each NotFound
Idempotency is achieved by the natural Delete + re-Get loop, not by separate guard flags or retries inside the handler

Blocked-condition message is the only operator-facing surface for the stuck-PushSecret state; no Warning event is emitted (the requeue itself + condition + V(1) log are the signals — avoids event storms across requeues)

issues_found.title: Tautological constant test should be removed
issues_found.description: TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue asserts that the package-level constant keystoneOpenBaoFinalizer equals the literal string 'keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer'. Both the constant declaration in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:38 and the test body are hardcoded copies of the same literal. The test cannot fail unless someone edits the constant AND forgets to update the test in the same PR, which the test itself does not meaningfully guard against. The actual behavioral guarantees that matter — that Reconcile installs the finalizer, that the finalizer name is stable across the chainsaw assertion (`contains(finalizers, 'keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/openbao-finalizer')`) and the docs (line 691) — are already covered by TestReconcile_AddsOpenBaoFinalizerOnFirstReconcile, the integration tests, and the chainsaw assertion respectively. The external review pattern .planwerk/review_patterns/reject-tautological-constant-tests.md has flagged this exact shape twice in past PRs (CC-0067 and CC-0078). For CC-0078 the equivalent TestKeystoneFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue was deleted on review.
issues_found.recommendation: Delete TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue (lines 1496-1504, plus the leading comment block) to stay consistent with the CC-0078 cleanup and the documented review pattern.

suggested_improvements.title: Consider asserting the finalizer release event ordering in NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue
suggested_improvements.description: TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue currently asserts FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets is emitted exactly once across two passes while the PushSecret stays Terminating. It does not, however, assert that OpenBaoSecretsFinalized is NOT emitted in either pass (the finalizer is not released because done=false on both passes). Adding a matching `g.Expect(...).NotTo(ContainElement(ContainSubstring('OpenBaoSecretsFinalized')))` would make the test's intent explicit and guard against a regression that flips the dedupe but loses the release-ordering invariant.

suggested_improvements.title: Reviewer-attention DECISION comment in chainsaw test should be resolved before merge
suggested_improvements.description: Step 6 contains a DECISION comment asking the reviewer to confirm openbao-system is the intended pod namespace (the task spec said 'openbao' but the deployed StatefulSet runs in openbao-system). The cited references (deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml:14 and deploy/openbao/bootstrap/common.sh:17) make the choice clearly correct, but the comment itself reads as an unresolved review pivot. Per .planwerk/review_patterns/resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge, replace the 'Reviewer: please verify' wording with a definitive note (e.g. 'See deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml — the OpenBao server runs in openbao-system; the task spec namespace 'openbao' is stale.') so the comment documents the resolved decision rather than soliciting one.

suggested_improvements.title: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets could surface stuckName via the blocked-condition helper signature
suggested_improvements.description: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets returns (done, stuckName, err) and only the immediate caller in reconcileDeleteOpenBao uses stuckName for the V(1) log line — the condition is set inside the function via setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition. The split between 'set the condition here, return the name to the caller for logging' is a minor coupling smell. A small refactor that either (a) moves the V(1) log inside finalizeOpenBaoSecrets, or (b) moves the SetCondition call into reconcileDeleteOpenBao alongside the V(1) log, would centralize the blocked-state handling. Optional polish — current structure works correctly.

next_steps: Delete TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go:1496-1504) per the reject-tautological-constant-tests review pattern.
Optionally apply the three suggested improvements above (test-intent assertion, chainsaw decision-comment cleanup, blocked-condition centralization) — none block merge.

Live-cluster verification of the chainsaw step 6 (`bao kv get` against both kv-v2 paths) is required before tagging the feature done — the sandbox cannot execute kubectl exec against the openbao-0 pod. Run `make e2e-test KEYSTONE_E2E_TESTS=tests/e2e/keystone/deletion-cleanup` against a kind cluster with openbao-system deployed and confirm both paths report 'absent in OpenBao' on the first run.

Verify that envtest is configured in CI to actually exercise TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove and TestIntegration_OpenBaoFinalizer_BlockedWhenPushSecretStuck — they Skip silently when KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS is unset.

detected_patterns.name: kubernetes-event-emission-alongside-condition-setting-with-fakerecorder-test-assertions
detected_patterns.evidence: FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets/OpenBaoSecretsFinalized Normal events emitted at the same lifecycle transitions as the SecretsReady condition is updated, asserted via record.NewFakeRecorder(10) drained into a slice and matched with ContainElement(ContainSubstring(...)) in 4 Reconcile-level tests.

detected_patterns.name: sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration
detected_patterns.evidence: setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition sets Type/Status/ObservedGeneration/Reason/Message on SecretsReady (matches the 5-field contract); ObservedGeneration explicitly asserted in TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_SetsBlockedConditionOnStall.

detected_patterns.name: gomega-test-assertions-with-newgomegawitht
detected_patterns.evidence: All new unit and integration tests use g := NewGomegaWithT(t) (or NewGomega) and the Gomega DSL — consistent with reconcile_secrets_test.go, reconcile_fernet_test.go, and integration_test.go conventions.

detected_patterns.name: namespace-isolated-envtest-integration-tests-with-skipifenvtestunavailable
detected_patterns.evidence: Both new integration tests start with testutil.SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t), create a GenerateName namespace ('test-openbao-finalizer-' / 'test-openbao-blocked-'), and operate inside it for full isolation.

detected_patterns.name: chainsaw-e2e-test-suite-structure-with-jmespath-condition-assertions
detected_patterns.evidence: deletion-cleanup chainsaw test uses the existing 6-step structure with assert/error/script blocks; the new finalizer-presence assertion uses (contains(finalizers, '...')) JMESPath expressions consistent with the CC-0078 assertion immediately above it.

detected_patterns.name: include-correlation-fields-in-operator-log-entries
detected_patterns.evidence: finalizeOpenBaoSecrets builds logger via log.FromContext(ctx).WithValues('keystone', client.ObjectKeyFromObject(keystone)) and reconcileDeleteOpenBao logs 'pushsecret=<name>' on the V(1) blocked log line — both surfaces include CR identity for log correlation.

detected_patterns.name: avoid-hardcoded-lists-that-duplicate-a-dynamic-source
detected_patterns.evidence: openBaoBackupPushSecretNames is the single source of truth for the two backup names; both finalizeOpenBaoSecrets and hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets iterate it — adding a third backup is a one-line change. Mirrors the mariaDBResourceCtors approach from CC-0078.

detected_patterns.name: reject-tautological-constant-tests
detected_patterns.evidence: TestKeystoneOpenBaoFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue (keystone_controller_test.go:1496-1504) re-asserts the literal value of the constant declared at reconcile_secrets.go:38. Identical to the deleted-on-review TestKeystoneFinalizerConstant_HasExpectedValue from CC-0078. See WARNING in issues_found.

detected_patterns.name: ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-their-coverage-scope
detected_patterns.evidence: Test names (e.g. TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_NoDuplicateStartEventOnRequeue, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_RequeuesWhilePushSecretTerminating, TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_SetsBlockedConditionOnStall) describe both the precondition and the asserted invariant.

detected_patterns.name: validate-stated-acceptance-criteria-are-met
detected_patterns.evidence: REQ-001 (AddFinalizer), REQ-002 (Delete + RemoveFinalizer ordering), REQ-003 (NotFound tolerance / idempotency), REQ-004 (blocked-condition + requeue), REQ-005 (constant), REQ-006 (DeletionTimestamp branching skip), REQ-007 (event semantics), REQ-008 (DeletionPolicy=Delete), REQ-009 (status surface), REQ-010 (E2E chainsaw with bao kv get) — each has a corresponding assertion or implementation site cited in inline godoc/tests.

detected_patterns.name: check-documentation-for-internal-contradictions
detected_patterns.evidence: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md OpenBao Finalizer section names both KV-v2 paths (line 708-709), both PushSecrets (line 706-709), the constant (line 691), and the finalizer wiring through ESO's DeletionPolicy=Delete (line 731-765) — all consistent with reconcile_secrets.go:38, reconcile_fernet.go:339-365, reconcile_credential.go:330-356, and the chainsaw test step 6 paths.

detected_patterns.name: namespace-isolated-envtest-integration-tests-with-skipifenvtestunavailable
detected_patterns.evidence: Integration tests use GenerateName for namespace isolation and SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable per the existing CC-0078 integration test pattern.
